Title: Modern plant metabolomics: advanced natural product gene discoveries, improved technologies, and future prospects

Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a marker/entry-point gene for engineering proanthocyanidin composition and seed coat traits, but polymerization/transport steps must be co-engineered for substantial end-product redesign.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes epicatechin formation and thereby supports proanthocyanidin accumulation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis with targeted metabolic profiling
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Not specified in this review; implicated in pale yellow seed coat mutants
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted flavonoid/proanthocyanidin metabolite profiling; molecular analysis of mutants
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review states this enzyme acts in the cytosol; downstream glycosylation, vacuolar transport, and laccase-like polymerization remain incompletely resolved.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential alteration of pigmentation and seed coat properties; not explicitly reported
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Anthocyanidin reductase is described as a key enzyme in proanthocyanidin biosynthesis. 2. It reduces cyanidin to epicatechin in the cytosol. 3. This reaction supplies the flavan-3-ol unit used for condensed tannin formation.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for pathway stacking with beta-amyrin synthase, CYP72A154, and downstream UDP-glucuronosyltransferases to engineer glycyrrhizin production in plants or heterologous hosts.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes two sequential oxidations at the C-11 methylene of beta-amyrin to produce an 11-oxo intermediate.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes conversion of beta-amyrin toward glycyrrhetinic acid/glycyrrhizin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expressed sequence tag analysis; gene expression analysis; metabolic profiling; functional enzyme characterization
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms intermediate precursor on the direct route to the target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Identified by correlation of expression with glycyrrhizin-related metabolism; detailed tissue pattern not specified in this review
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Expressed sequence tags; gene expression analysis; metabolic profiling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as an early oxidative tailoring enzyme after beta-amyrin formation.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Beta-amyrin oxidative tailoring branch toward glycyrrhetinic acid
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum/microsome (typical for P450s; not explicitly stated in review)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP88D6 was identified through integrated sequence, expression, and metabolic analyses in licorice. 2. It catalyzes sequential C-11 oxidation of beta-amyrin. 3. This establishes a direct oxidative step toward glycyrrhetinic acid and glycyrrhizin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal sweet triterpenoid with pharmaceutical and bioactive properties

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value engineering target for constructing glycyrrhetinic acid/glycyrrhizin pathways; should be combined with upstream beta-amyrin supply and downstream glycosylation modules.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Performs three-step oxidation at the C-30 methyl of beta-amyrin-derived intermediates to generate a carboxylic acid en route to glycyrrhetinic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes oxidation of beta-amyrin pathway intermediates toward glycyrrhetinic acid and glycyrrhizin.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene expression analysis; metabolic profiling; functional enzyme characterization
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor/direct intermediate toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression correlated with glycyrrhizin pathway activity; exact tissue pattern not specified in this review
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review notes CYP72A63 from Medicago truncatula performs a similar C-30 oxidation, implying CYP72A subfamily specialization in triterpene oxidation.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Beta-amyrin oxidative tailoring branch toward glycyrrhetinic acid
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Glycyrrhetinic acid or direct oxidized C-30 intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum/microsome (typical for P450s; not explicitly stated in review)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP72A154 was identified as a triterpene-oxidizing P450 in licorice. 2. It catalyzes three-step C-30 oxidation. 3. This reaction yields the carboxyl functionality required for glycyrrhetinic acid formation on the route to glycyrrhizin.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal sweet triterpenoid with pharmaceutical and bioactive properties

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate enzyme for installing C-28 oxidation in engineered triterpene pathways for oleanolic/ursolic/betulinic acid derivatives.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidation at the C-28 position of beta-amyrin, alpha-amyrin, and lupeol.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of oxidized triterpene acids used in saponin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms intermediate precursor on route to oxidized triterpene end products
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Described as multifunctional P450 with broad triterpene substrate range.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: C-28 oxidation branch of triterpene metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad substrate activity may cause off-target triterpene shifts if engineered
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Oleanolic acid/ursolic acid/betulinic acid-type oxidized products
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum/microsome (typical for P450s; not explicitly stated in review)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP716A12 is a multifunctional triterpene oxidase. 2. It oxidizes multiple triterpene scaffolds at C-28. 3. It broadens the routes to oxidized sapogenin backbones for saponin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Various tissues depending on species; not specified in this review

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for pathway completion when engineering hederagenin-derived saponins; should be combined with scaffold-forming and oxidizing genes.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Glucosylates hederagenin at the C-28 position.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes glycosylated triterpene saponin formation from hederagenin.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Identified from co-expressed genes; exact tissue pattern not specified
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter saponin profile broadly if overexpressed
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytosol (typical for UGTs; not explicitly stated)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT73F3 was identified from a co-expression set in Medicago truncatula. 2. It glucosylates hederagenin at C-28. 3. This defines a downstream tailoring step in triterpene saponin biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for modifying sapogenin glycosylation patterns in defense-oriented metabolic engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 3-O-glucosylation of oleanolic acid and hederagenin.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paralogous with UGT73C11 in Barbarea vulgaris.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential broad effects on sapogenin glycoside spectrum
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytosol (typical for UGTs; not explicitly stated)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT73C10 glucosylates sapogenins at the 3-O position. 2. It accepts oleanolic acid and hederagenin. 3. It contributes to triterpene saponin diversification.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for modifying sapogenin glycosylation patterns in defense-oriented metabolic engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 3-O-glucosylation of oleanolic acid and hederagenin.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paralogous with UGT73C10 in Barbarea vulgaris.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential broad effects on sapogenin glycoside spectrum
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytosol (typical for UGTs; not explicitly stated)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT73C11 glucosylates sapogenins at the 3-O position. 2. It acts on oleanolic acid and hederagenin. 3. It contributes to triterpene saponin diversification.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for tuning soyasaponin A glycoside structures in soybean quality engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Glucosylates the soyasapogenol A aglycone at the C-22 position.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of C-22 glucosylated soyasaponin derivatives.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Close similarity to UGT73F4; described as allelic genes.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter seed saponin composition and sensory traits; not explicitly discussed
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytosol (typical for UGTs; not explicitly stated)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT73F2 was identified through soybean mutant biochemistry. 2. It glucosylates the C-22 position of soyasapogenol A. 3. It defines a specific late tailoring step in soyasaponin A biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense and seed quality-associated specialized metabolites

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for tuning soyasaponin A glycoside structures in soybean quality engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Xylosylates the soyasapogenol A aglycone at the C-22 position.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of C-22 xylosylated soyasaponin derivatives.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Close similarity to UGT73F2; described as allelic genes.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter seed saponin composition and sensory traits; not explicitly discussed
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytosol (typical for UGTs; not explicitly stated)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT73F4 was identified through soybean mutant biochemistry. 2. It xylosylates the C-22 position of soyasapogenol A. 3. It defines a specific late tailoring step in soyasaponin A biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense and seed quality-associated specialized metabolites

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important early-pathway engineering target for reconstituting iridoid and downstream monoterpenoid indole alkaloid production.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of the iridoid scaffold as a NAD(P)H-dependent oxidoreductase-like enzyme.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports biosynthesis of iridoid precursors required for secologanin and downstream monoterpenoid indole alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Integrated transcriptome-metabolome discovery followed by functional characterization
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review does not provide the specific gene symbol in this summary passage.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Seco-iridoid branch feeding monoterpenoid indole alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: First committed scaffold-forming step implied, but not explicitly designated rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A NAD(P)H-dependent oxidoreductase-like enzyme was identified in Catharanthus roseus. 2. It catalyzes formation of the iridoid scaffold. 3. This provides the monoterpenoid branch precursor for monoterpenoid indole alkaloid biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for reconstructing secologanin supply in monoterpenoid indole alkaloid engineering platforms.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose to 7-deoxyloganetic acid in secologanin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes further steps toward secologanin formation and downstream monoterpenoid indole alkaloid production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Combined transcriptome-metabolome discovery followed by verification via heterologous expression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review highlights completion of all genes for the seco-iridoid pathway in Catharanthus by integrated omics and heterologous validation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A 7-deoxyloganetic acid glucosyltransferase was identified in Catharanthus roseus. 2. It acts in secologanin biosynthesis. 3. This supports precursor formation for monoterpenoid indole alkaloids.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Foundational entry-step gene for engineering benzylisoquinoline alkaloids and morphinan pathways; should be coordinated with downstream tailoring genes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Not extracted due to review summary ambiguity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Targeted metabolite profiling and omics-assisted pathway characterization
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Alkaloid biosynthetic enzyme transcripts correlate with alkaloid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review notes that most genes in the morphine pathway have been identified, but does not enumerate individual genes in the provided text; therefore no specific morphine enzyme names are confidently extractable here.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The review states that most genes in the morphine pathway have been identified using omics and targeted profiling. 2. However, individual genes are not listed in the provided passage. 3. Therefore no single specific pathway enzyme can be confidently detailed from this text alone.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Latex/capsules (general species knowledge; not explicitly stated here)

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical committed-step gene for engineering camptothecin production; suppression experiments indicate strong leverage over intermediate and pathway flux.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Decarboxylates tryptophan to form tryptamine, the first committed step in alkaloid biosynthesis branch studied.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports camptothecin pathway flux by supplying tryptamine precursor.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA interference lines; metabolomics profiling of intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Comparison between camptothecin-producing hairy roots and non-producing suspension cells
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differentially associated with camptothecin-producing hairy roots versus non-producing cells
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as one of the genes suppressed by RNAi to define intermediate candidates in the pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Tryptamine branch feeding monoterpenoid indole alkaloid/camptothecin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Suppression likely reduces alkaloid accumulation; no broader fitness effects reported
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tryptophan decarboxylase was used as a committed-step marker in camptothecin biosynthesis studies. 2. RNAi suppression altered pathway intermediates detected by metabolomics. 3. This confirmed its causal role in supplying alkaloid precursor flux.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical committed-pathway gene for engineering secologanin supply to camptothecin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes a first committed reaction in secologanin biosynthesis branch supporting camptothecin formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports camptothecin pathway flux by supplying secologanin precursor branch.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA interference lines; metabolomics profiling of intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Comparison between camptothecin-producing hairy roots and non-producing suspension cells
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differentially associated with camptothecin-producing hairy roots versus non-producing cells
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Suppression helped delineate intermediate candidates in pathway reconstruction.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Suppression likely reduces alkaloid accumulation; no broader fitness effects reported
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450 or oxidative enzyme class in iridoid pathway (not specified here)
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: First committed enzyme in the branch, as described in review
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Secologanin synthase was one of the first committed enzymes targeted by RNAi in Ophiorrhiza pumila. 2. Metabolomic changes in RNAi lines delineated candidate intermediates. 3. This verified its role in precursor supply for camptothecin biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Major target for reducing quinolizidine alkaloids in lupin breeding or introducing cadaverine-derived alkaloid pathways in heterologous systems; editing/downregulation may help create low-alkaloid varieties.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Decarboxylates lysine to cadaverine, the first committed step in most lysine-derived alkaloid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes cadaverine and downstream lysine-derived alkaloid accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Differential gene expression analysis between bitter and sweet varieties; overexpression in Arabidopsis and tobacco cells; metabolomic profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Comparison of alkaloid-accumulating bitter versus alkaloid-less sweet varieties
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In vivo function confirmed by detection of cadaverine in Arabidopsis and cadaverine-derived tobacco alkaloids in tobacco cells expressing the gene.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reducing this gene may lower defensive alkaloids; overexpression can create non-native alkaloid shifts
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Lysine decarboxylase was identified by differential expression between bitter and sweet lupin varieties. 2. It catalyzes the first committed step from lysine to cadaverine. 3. Overexpression in Arabidopsis and tobacco validated its in vivo capacity to generate cadaverine-derived metabolites.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defense; impacts crop quality and toxicity

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising regulator for coordinated glucosinolate engineering because it can control multiple pathway genes; deployment should consider sulfur nutrition context and defense-growth balance.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes glucosinolate biosynthesis under sulfate-deficient stress by regulating pathway gene expression.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Integrated transcriptome-metabolome analysis under sulfate deficiency; multivariate data mining; candidate gene identification
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Described as a transcription factor pinpointed by integrated omics as a key candidate involved in glucosinolate biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Nutrient-responsive regulation implied; specific feedback/feedforward loop not reported
Regulation_Genes.Gene_Name: MYB transcription factor (glucosinolate-related; unspecified in review passage)
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome; metabolome; BL-SOM analysis; co-expression network analyses with public microarray data
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The specific MYB family member is not named in the provided passage, so precise accession and direct targets cannot be confidently assigned.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential growth-defense tradeoff and sulfur allocation effects; not explicitly detailed
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Glucosinolate biosynthetic genes (specific targets not named in this review passage)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Regulates expression of glucosinolate pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus (expected for TF; not explicitly stated)
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Integrated transcriptome-metabolome analyses under sulfate deficiency pinpointed a glucosinolate-related MYB transcription factor. 2. This regulator was identified as a key candidate controlling glucosinolate biosynthesis. 3. Its discovery exemplified how metabolomics accelerates identification of pathway regulators.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Arabidopsis vegetative tissues/seeds depending compound; not specified here
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense compounds important against pests and pathogens
